feedback.text: When I buy a product, I want to own it. If it has an expiry date, I would like to know it before buying it. If I obtain a product licence for office, the manufacturer can easily tell me how long I can use the product. If I buy a game, why does it not work anymore? What is the difference? Is this in the general terms and conditions? If so, this is a licence and not a product if Ubisoft says that we can no longer have games, then they should also advertise.
